TextENGNew: The Seventh Mart was a discount liquor and grocery store in the city.
I piled a colorful assortment of cup noodles into the cart, grumbling back at my parents as they urged me to hurry up.
Sure enough, Mom let out an exasperated sigh, asking why I needed so many different kinds.
``Cup noodles have gotten really elaborate lately, and there are so many varieties. I want to try each one at least once.``
I knew I was being a bit selfish, but I figured it was worth a shot.
Overwhelmed by the sheer quantity and variety, Dad frowned and told me to go pick out a case instead of buying them individually.
Well, I’d seen this coming. But once Dad weighed in, there was no arguing.
``Then I’d be stuck with just one kind! I’d get sick of it!``
It was a token resistance.
In my heart, I’d already given up and was trying to decide which case of noodles to get.
Mom and Dad had already gotten in line at the register, calling over to me as I lingered at the shelves, still agonizing.
The line wasn’t long—I was running out of time... With no choice left, I quickly located the case I wanted.
When in doubt... that’s right! Pork-bone and ginger, extra-large!
If I left it to Mom, she’d play it safe and go for soy sauce or salt flavor every time.
So when I plunked a case of pork-bone flavor into the cart, she looked back at me with an expression that said, "...These?"
But this was my hill to die on, and I launched into my defense of the selection.
``Pork-bone is delicious! It’s a big serving, sure, but the flavor isn’t overpowering...!!``
In my recollection, I was passionately arguing for the rightness of my chosen noodles.
In this world of finished time, already sealed and laminated, there was no way I could look around...
changeReason: New translation preserves the specific 'laminated' metaphor (ラミネートでパッキング) for the sealed-off nature of past time, while original uses the vaguer 'frozen/encapsulated.'

TextENGNew: So all I could do was sharpen my sight, hearing, and awareness of presences within this moment.
No matter how thoroughly I searched my field of vision... I couldn’t find Rena.
I rewound and searched again.
But of course, I couldn’t find her.
Then... had she been watching me from outside my field of vision—from my blind spot...?
I went back through sounds and presences, searching once more.
The presences of other shoppers.
All of them moving about randomly... going about their business.
No one was watching me intently, no one was trailing behind me.
Nothing.@kNothing there.@kProbably nothing.
Even as careless as I was back then... I would absolutely have noticed if someone were right on my heels.
I smiled wryly at myself for using the vague word "probably" and then immediately contradicting it with "absolutely."
At that moment, a chill ran through me, and I stopped the replay.
...There was definitely a presence—like a shadow—behind me.
The terror was... beyond description.
If a presence had actually appeared behind the real me, I could have turned around to check.
But trapped in this world of time already past, I could not turn around...
changeReason: New translation preserves the specific concept of 'world of time already past' (終わってしまった時間の世界), while original vaguely says 'the world had moved on.'

TextENGNew: Bearing that terrifying shadow on my back... had I really been cheerfully running through the store, searching for a case of cup noodles...?
Dashing around the instant noodle section, complaining about Mom...
But... behind me, the presence was always there. Clinging to me like a shadow.
The fact that I could not verify it—that I could only become aware of it now, after the fact—was this terrifying, this revolting...
In this world of finished time... I was running through it, full of cheer.
Clutching a cardboard box.
Pitter-patter.
...But listening more carefully to those footsteps... they unmistakably contained something else, a sound apart from my own footfalls—a soft padding.
Pitter-patter, pitter-patter, pitter-patter.@kPad-pad-pad.
Pitter-patter.@kPad-pad.
Pitter-patter, pitter-patter.@kPad-pad-pad.
Matching my running exactly, those soft, almost barefoot-sounding steps followed behind me.
In this world of finished time... I was running through it, full of cheer.
But I couldn’t hear it—at least, I hadn’t at the time.
No. I had heard it. That’s why I could remember it now.
...I had heard it, but hadn’t paid it any mind.
changeReason: New translation correctly renders 聞こえていたが気にしなかった as 'I had heard it but hadn't paid it any mind,' while original incorrectly states 'I didn't think I had heard anything,' contradicting the JPN.

TextENGNew: That’s why I didn’t turn around.
That is why... I cannot turn around...!!!
changeReason: New translation correctly uses 'cannot turn around' (matching JPN potential form 振り返られない), while original uses past tense 'didn't turn around,' missing the present inability.

TextENGNew: In this world of finished time, those padding footsteps had been pursuing me the entire while.
I couldn’t run faster to escape.
In this finished time, I could only run at the speed I had already run.
I couldn’t turn around.
Because in that finished time, I had never once looked behind me.
And then I reached my parents and began to speak.@kAll while that shadow-like presence clung to my back.
Because I wasn’t moving, the shadow wasn’t moving either.@kSo there was no sound.@kThat was all.
At that time, I had been standing perfectly still while talking to my parents.
Completely motionless.@kThat much was certain.
And yet—@k@|@yThat shouldn’t be possible.
If I took three steps, it followed for three.@kThat was supposed to be the rule...?
After that, there were no more sounds.
And then the entire world lost power.@k...Everything went pitch black.
changeReason: New translation correctly renders 停電 as 'lost power' (a power outage), while original uses 'gone dark,' missing the specific cause.

TextENGNew: My journey through memory was over.
I was exhausted.
I wanted to stop.
Someone, turn on the lights.
But my body wouldn’t move.
...As if I had been stitched into... that world of finished time.
Pad.
Every hair on my body stood on end.
changeReason: New translation correctly renders 全身の毛が逆立つ as 'every hair on my body stood on end,' while original adds 'past self's' and limits it to head hair, diverging from the JPN.

TextENGNew: This can’t be...?!
It’s been breaking the rules over and over!!
changeReason: New translation captures さっきから...ばかり (has been nothing but rule-breaking since earlier), conveying repeated violations, while original treats it as a single current violation.

TextENGNew: I’m not moving!
So you shouldn’t be moving either!!
I can’t move!@k So you shouldn’t be able to move either!!@k Follow the rules!!!
Pad.
And yet, that sound echoed through the darkness once more.
The hair at the back of my head prickled.
It was so close behind me... close enough that I couldn’t tell if it was brushing against my hair or not.
Why could the presence behind me move while I could not?!?!
...The answer came immediately.
I could move.
...I was simply too terrified to turn around.
Now was the only chance I had to look.
An act that could never be permitted in a world of finished time...@kBut... if I didn’t turn around now...!!
Every cell in my body protested—as if needles were being driven into every last pore, trying to stop me from committing this forbidden act...
I’ll turn around!
I’ll turn around!
I’m not afraid!!
I’ll turn around!
I’ll turn around!
I’m not afraid!!!!
It was a war cry that I could not voice—a scream trapped within my chest.
OoOoOoOOOOOOoOoOOh!!!!!
I turned around.
...What was there... at first, I could not comprehend its meaning.
This is... what...?
Just as a mouth might bite into an apple, taste the juice, and only then recognize it as an apple... my brain began to process the scene before my eyes by "eating the apple."
Crunch, crunch... chewing...
Tasting the juice... and recognizing the apple.
In other words... what lay before my eyes was—
GYAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHH......
